##NAMES=Clinochalcomenite
##RRUFFID=R110125
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CuSe^4+^O_3_(H_2_O)_2_
##LOCALITY=El Dragon mine, Potosi, Bolivia
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Royal Ontario Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of pale greenish-blue crystals associated with darker blue chalcomenite
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110125
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Cu_0.99_Ni_0.02_Co_0.01_Fe_0.01_)Se^+4^_1.11_O_3_·1.77H_2_O ; Note: water content determined by difference
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.283 b: 8.607 c: 8.143 alpha: 90 beta: 97.337 gamma: 90 volume: 436.8 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
